title: F-002 @clack 包上下文 wrap API (themeIntro/outro/log/note)
description: Create packages/cli/src/install/theme-clack.ts: a thin wrap of @clack/prompts CONTEXT output (intro/outro/log/note) that re-renders it through the W3-B structural primitives — themeIntro = section-bar (▌ accent bold) + rule line; themeOutro = success/accent closing line; themeLog.info|success|warn|error = status badge + paint; themeNote = left-bar (│) grouped block. Per C-006 this ONLY wraps context — prompt controls (text/select/confirm/multiselect) stay @clack default and MUST NOT be touched. Ship a NO_COLOR snapshot of the four wrap outputs.
action: Create packages/cli/src/install/theme-clack.ts exporting: `themeIntro(title: string): void` → writes sectionBar(title) + a muted rule line (─── / -----); `themeOutro(msg: string): void` → writes a success-painted closing line; `themeLog = { info, success, warn, error }` each `(msg: string) => void` → writes `${symbol/badge} ${paint(role, msg)}`; `themeNote(body: string, title?: string): void` → writes a left-bar (│ / |) grouped block with optional sectionBar title. Import sectionBar from @fenglimg/fabric-shared/theme, symbol/paint/isColorEnabled via colors.ts, and the grid rule helper from ../tui/structure.js. Each fn writes via process.stdout.write. Extract the pure string builders (buildIntro/buildOutro/buildLog/buildNote) so the snapshot test can assert NO_COLOR output without spying on stdout.

criteria: packages/cli/src/install/theme-clack.ts exports themeIntro, themeOutro, themeLog, themeNote (grep -E 'export (function|const) (themeIntro|themeOutro|themeLog|themeNote)' returns all four)
NO @clack prompt control is wrapped: grep -E 'text\(|select\(|confirm\(|multiselect\(' packages/cli/src/install/theme-clack.ts returns 0
a NO_COLOR snapshot test for buildIntro/buildOutro/buildLog/buildNote exists and passes (toMatchSnapshot, NO_COLOR=1)
convergence.verification: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- theme-clack && pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit
convergence.definition_of_done: @clack context output (intro/outro/log/note) renders through the W3-B primitives via a thin wrap; prompt controls are untouched; the wrap output is snapshot-pinned.

files.change: Context-only @clack wrap using sectionBar + badge/paint + left-bar group; pure build* helpers for snapshotting.

implementation: Inspect install-wizard.ts to see how @clack intro/outro/log/note are currently called (so the wrap is a drop-in for the context calls only).
Create theme-clack.ts with pure build* string builders + thin void wrappers that write to stdout.

buildIntro = sectionBar(title) + '\n' + muted rule line; buildOutro = paint('success'|'accent', msg); buildLog.<level> = badge/symbol + paint(role,msg); buildNote = title sectionBar? + body lines each prefixed with painted '│ ' (ASCII '| ').
Add theme-clack.test.ts: NO_COLOR=1 snapshot of all builders.

reference.pattern: pure render fn + establish a NEW vitest toMatchSnapshot test with NO_COLOR=1 (grouped-help.test.ts uses toContain/toMatch, not toMatchSnapshot, and does not pin NO_COLOR — do not reuse it; add a fresh NO_COLOR=1 snapshot test)

rationale.chosen_approach: Wrap only the @clack context surface (intro/outro/log/note) per C-006 — restyling prompt controls is an explicit non-goal. Pure build* helpers make the wrap snapshot-testable without stdout spies.
rationale.tradeoffs: Two call styles coexist (themed context + raw @clack controls) — accepted; this is the locked scope.

risks: Accidentally importing/wrapping a control fn would violate C-006 — guarded by the grep convergence criterion.
